sanskrit:
आत्मौपम्येन सर्वत्र समं पश्यति योऽर्जुन।
सुखं वा यदि वा दुःखं स योगी परमो मतः॥
transliteration:
ātmaupamyena sarvatra samaṃ paśyati yo ’rjuna |
sukhaṃ vā yadi vā duḥkhaṃ sa yogī paramo mataḥ ||
literal_meaning: By the standard of his own self he sees everywhere equally—whether joy or sorrow—such a yogi is deemed the highest.
contextual_meaning: True psychological maturity is the capacity to transpose one’s inner experience onto others, recognizing that every being’s pleasure and pain are as real and urgent as one’s own. This empathic equation dissolves the illusion of separateness and is the hallmark of integrated selfhood.
psychological_state: Post-conventional empathic identity: the self is no longer the center of the emotional universe; every sentient perspective is included in one’s circle of care without bias.
trigger_scenario: A manager must lay off half her team; instead of cold efficiency she mentally swaps places with each member, feeling their mortgage panic and shame, then designs severance and retraining that treats their distress as seriously as she would her own.
anchor_text: Feel others' joy and pain as your own.
